description: You ease the cell door open and step into a narrow passage just outside the prison block. Another locked door stands ahead, and beside it someone has scratched a line into the stone: I speak without a mouth. I hear without ears. I have no body but come alive with the wind.

After getting past the warden, you reach an iron gate that blocks the rest of the stairway. A brass plaque is fixed to it: ONLY THOSE WHO UNDERSTAND ORDER MAY PASS. Under the message is a calculation. If you answer badly, the mechanism will punish you.
You enter the library side passage and find a sealed inner door. The latch is built around four scratched letters: T E A G. It looks like a simple word puzzle, but it is the only way forward.

Past the library and kitchen routes, you reach another sealed way deeper into the castle. A dry voice speaks through the stone before the passage opens: The more you take, the more you leave behind. Ravenspire tests your mind as often as your strength.

You climb into the upper tower route, but a stone slab drops across the staircase and blocks the way. Carved into it is a message: SOLVE THIS TO ASCEND. FAIL AND REMAIN. Beneath it is one final calculation.

After surviving the side hall or the grand hall, you reach a glowing vault door that guards the royal archive. A voice comes from inside the stone itself: I have keys but no locks. Space but no room. You can enter but never go inside. Answer correctly and the archive opens.

Near the gate, a black door covered in glowing runes blocks the final safe route out. A voice asks one last question, as if the castle would rather trap you here than let you leave with what you have learned: I have cities but no houses. Mountains but no trees. Rivers but no water. I am real but cannot be touched.
